case_title: JOHN JENKEL, A SAN FRANCISCO PROPERTY OWNER VS. LS CAPITAL GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JOHN JENKEL, A SAN FRANCISCO PROPERTY OWNER'S PETITION FOR EXTRAORDINARY RELIEF FROM DOMESTIC TERRORISM IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 17, 2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE ALL PARTIES THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PETITIONER AND RESPONDENT MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE PETITION AND OPPOSITION, RESPECTIVELY, TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 7, 2012 AT 4:00 PM. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR EXTRAORDINARY RELIEF FROM DOMESTIC TERRORISM IS DENIED. WRIT IMPROPER UNDER CCP 1102 WHICH ADDRESSES JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS. ALTERNATIVELY IF VIEWED AS REQUEST FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION, FAILS TO SHOW LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. COUNSEL FOR RESPONDENT TO PREPARE ORDER IN CONFORMITY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. SMITH, NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Petitioners Petition For Extraordinary Relief From Domestic Terrorism
Off Calendar Dec-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER JOHN JENKEL, A SAN FRANCISCO PROPERTY OWNER'S Petition For Extraordinary Relief From Domestic Terrorism will be heard in Department 501 on the same day and time. Judge: Marla Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Petitioner'S Request For Extraordinary Relief From Domestic Terrorism
Off Calendar Dec-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jenkel (707-732-3464) Petitioner and moving party in Pro Per / Courtney Kay (310-478-2541) of Glickfeld & Nass, Inc. for LS Capital Group, LLC, respondent and opposing party

ARGUED - The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Petitioner's Request For Extraordinary Relief From Domestic Terrorism is denied. Writ improper under CCP 1102 which addresses judicial proceedings. Alternatively if viewed as request for Preliminary Injunction, fails to show likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Counsel for respondent to prepare order in conformity with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Smith, Not reported (D501)

Ntc Of Petitioners Petition For Extraordinary Relief From Domestic Terrorism
Off Calendar Dec-07-2012 Continued To Dec-17-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Jenkel, petitioner In Pro Per.
Jerry L. Kay, Esq., 310.478.2541, for Respondent LS Capital Group, LLC.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER JOHN JENKEL, A SAN FRANCISCO PROPERTY OWNER'S Petition For Extraordinary Relief From Domestic Terrorism is continued to December 17, 2012 to give all parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Petitioner and Respondent must deliver courtesy copies of the Petition and opposition, respectively, to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than December 7, 2012 at 4:00 pm. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
